---
title: "Maintainer Interview Guide"
subtitle: "GenAI Usage and Burden"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: false
  typst:
    margin:
      x: 0.55in
      y: 0.55in
    fontsize: 10pt
    number-sections: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        #show heading.where(level: 1): set text(size: 13pt, weight: "bold")
        #show heading.where(level: 1): set block(above: 10pt, below: 4pt)
        #show heading.where(level: 2): set text(size: 11pt, weight: "bold")
        #show heading.where(level: 2): set block(above: 8pt, below: 3pt)
        #set list(spacing: 5pt)
        #set par(spacing: 6pt, leading: 0.6em)
---

## Purpose

This guide supports one on one, recorded interviews with open source package
maintainers.
The goal is to understand how GenAI and LLM tools are affecting maintainer
workload and burden, not just how often people use these tools.
Findings will feed into a pyOpenSci writeup and reporting effort on overall
maintainer burden.

## How to use this guide

Treat this as semi-structured, not a script.
The core section is designed to run about 30 minutes.
If the conversation is going well and there is time, move into the extension
section, which can extend the conversation to about 60 minutes.
Follow interesting threads even if they mean skipping ahead or out of order.
Probes are suggestions, not requirements. Use them when a question lands flat
or when there is room to go deeper.

## Intro and consent (about 2 minutes)

Read or paraphrase:

"Thanks for making time for this. This is part of a pyOpenSci effort looking
at how GenAI tools are affecting package maintainers, specifically around
workload and burden, not just adoption rates.
I would like to record this so I can stay in the conversation instead of
taking notes.
The recording will be used for transcription and analysis, and any published
reporting will anonymize your responses.
Is that okay with you?

We have about 30 minutes, maybe up to 60 if it is flowing and you have time.
There are no wrong answers here. I am interested in your honest experience,
including the parts that feel messy or contradictory."

## Stay involved

Want to stay involved after this interview? Scan the code or use the link
below to join the survey, sprint, or focus group mailing list.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
::: {style="border:2px dashed #888; width:160px; height:160px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; text-align:center; margin:1.5em auto; font-size:0.9em; color:#666;"}
QR code placeholder
:::
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="typst"}
```{=typst}
#align(center)[
  #box(width: 1.3in, height: 1.3in, stroke: 1pt, inset: 8pt)[
    #align(center + horizon)[QR CODE #linebreak() PLACEHOLDER]
  ]
]
```
:::

Survey link: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

```{=typst}
#pagebreak()
#set page(columns: 2)
```

## Core block (about 30 minutes)

### Context and workflow (about 5 minutes)

- What project or projects do you maintain, and how long have you been doing
  it?
- How big is your maintainer team? How much of this lands on you alone?
- Where does GenAI show up in your day to day maintenance work right now, if
  at all? Think coding, review, triage, docs, communications.

Probe: if they say GenAI does not show up in their own work, ask whether they
see it showing up in others' contributions even so.

### Motivations and perceptions (about 7 minutes)

- What led you to start using GenAI tools in your maintainer work? Or, what
  has kept you from adopting them?
- When you do use them, what are you actually gaining? Not how often you use
  them, but what the value is.
- What are your honest feelings about these tools in your role as a
  maintainer specifically?
- Has that perception shifted over time? Better, worse, more mixed?

Probe: is there a gap between what you think you are supposed to feel about
these tools and what you actually feel day to day?

### Burden and strain (about 8 minutes)

- Where has GenAI added burden to your work? Where has it relieved it?
- Walk me through a recent time you reviewed a contribution you suspected was
  AI assisted. What was that like?
- How much of your current strain, if any, do you attribute to GenAI related
  changes versus everything else?
- Is there a specific moment that stands out as particularly stressful in
  this space?

Probe: this can be a hard thing to say out loud. If someone hesitates,
reiterate that this is confidential and anonymized in any writeup.

### Perfect world and desired change (about 8 minutes)

- In a perfect world, what would contributions to your project look like,
  given these tools exist?
- What is the one thing that, if changed, would most reduce your burden right
  now?
- What would you need, from tools, from the community, or from other
  maintainers, to feel less strained?
- If you could send one message to every contributor using GenAI to submit to
  your project, what would it be?

*This is a natural stopping point if time is short.*

```{=typst}
#colbreak()
```

## Extension block (about 30 more minutes, time permitting)

### AI-generated contribution quality (about 7 to 8 minutes)

- How do you currently tell, or guess, when a contribution was AI generated
  or AI assisted?
- How has the volume of AI-influenced contributions changed compared to a
  year or two ago?
- Does your triage process differ for these versus other contributions? Walk
  me through it.
- Are there specific incidents with low quality or hallucinated AI-generated
  submissions that stick with you?

### Tool-specific deep dive (about 5 to 6 minutes)

- Which specific tools do you personally use? For example Copilot, ChatGPT,
  Claude Code, Cursor, or review bots like CodeRabbit.
- Walk me through a specific, recent example of using one of these in your
  maintainer work.
- Is there anything you tried and abandoned? What did not work?

### Before and after comparison (about 5 to 6 minutes)

- Think back to your workflow two or three years ago versus today. What is
  concretely different?
- Has the kind of work you do changed? For example, less writing code, more
  reviewing and verifying.

### Community and behavior change (about 5 to 6 minutes)

- What would help maintainers collectively, not just you personally? Think
  policies, norms, tooling.
- Are there other projects or communities you think are handling this well?
- What do you wish the broader open source or pyOpenSci community understood
  about this that is not well understood yet?

## Closing (about 2 to 3 minutes)

Read or paraphrase:

"Is there anything I have not asked about that you think is important here?

Thank you so much for your candor and your time, this is genuinely useful.
If you are interested, there is a survey and a focus group you can join, and
I can send you the link.
I will follow up once this is written up if you would like to see where it
lands."
